features.title: LocalStack DynamoDB CMS harness
features.description: Provide a local LocalStack DynamoDB setup that mimics the CMS data layer for the webapp and is usable by future coding agents.

evidence: npm run build in aws: passed
pnpm lint in sunswift-website-v3: passed with one existing unused Geist warning
./init.sh rerun on 2026-05-15: blocked at Docker daemon health check
./init.sh on 2026-05-15: passed after pinning LocalStack to 4.10.0 and adding idempotent S3 bootstrap

notes: LocalStack now starts with DynamoDB and S3 for the CMS harness.

features.title: Sunswift redesign foundation and Webflow text import
features.description: First redesign slice with public route shells, Opal-inspired homepage, Webflow text import, CMS v1 for team members and roles, S3 headshot staging, and Auth.js Google admin login.

evidence: ./init.sh: passed with LocalStack DynamoDB and S3
npm run build in aws: passed
Vehicles route contained Garage archive, Sunswift 7, and Related posts text
Admin login route contained Sunswift CMS, Admin login, and Continue with Google text
Follow-up visual verification enabled with local agent-browser script on 2026-05-15

notes: Webflow text is imported into repo-managed JSON via pnpm import:webflow.
CMS v1 is limited to team members, headshots, and recruitment roles.
Auth.js is configured for Google OAuth and authorized email gating.

features.title: Opal-inspired light homepage, browser verification, and README
features.description: Update the Sunswift homepage toward the Opal Tadpole reference with a light image-led hero, Roobert-like typography, Tomorrow Today slogan, documented human workflows, and repeatable agent-browser verification.

evidence: ./init.sh: passed with LocalStack DynamoDB and S3, AWS build/test, frontend typecheck/lint
pnpm verify:browser: passed after installing Chrome for agent-browser; desktop 1440x1000 and mobile 390x844 checked for content, dev overlays, screenshots, and horizontal overflow
HTTP smoke while dev server was running: /, /vehicles, and /admin/login returned 200

notes: agent-browser is now a local dev dependency with pnpm verify:browser.
Root README and app README document local running, testing, browser verification, and deploy testing.

features.title: Remove Opal naming, add transparent navbar and about section
features.description: Strip internal Opal references, rename the homepage hero, add a transparent overlay navbar with the Sunswift navigation, and extend the homepage with the Sunswift about copy and a placeholder image.
notes: Homepage hero renamed to HomepageHero and CSS hooks renamed to .homepage-hero-*.
Transparent navbar sits over the dark hero and links to all seven Sunswift sections.
About section uses placeholder garage.svg image until real Sunswift photography lands.

features.title: Typing slogan and Guinness records section
features.description: Add an Apple-style Guinness World Records section below the about block and animate the homepage slogan with a typing effect ending in 'Tomorrow, Today.'.
notes: Slogan now reveals one character at a time and ends with a blinking caret; an sr-only full slogan keeps accessibility intact.
Records section uses placeholder vehicle SVGs; swap with real photography when assets land.
Browser harness now waits for data-typing-complete before asserting the slogan and checks records copy.

features.title: Statement section, records kicker removed, vehicles full-page gallery
features.description: Drop the Guinness kicker, add an Opal-style big-text statement between hero and about, expand the vehicles dataset to all seven cars, and replace /vehicles with a hover-expand, click-to-open gallery that uses the whole page.
notes: Vehicles dataset now mirrors the Webflow source (Sunswift 7, VIolet, eVe, IVy, III, II, I).
Placeholder SVGs cover all seven cars; 3D renders are deferred until requested.
Vehicles detail view is client-state only — no /vehicles/[slug] route yet.

features.title: Monochrome theme with yellow accents and vehicles layout flip
features.description: Shift the site palette to black/white with a single Sunswift-yellow accent for important states (e.g. Join the team hover), flip the vehicles page so cards occupy the top and the headline anchors the bottom, and add staggered/blur transitions.
notes: Yellow exposed via --accent-yellow / Tailwind utilities (text-accent-yellow etc).
Vehicle gallery uses vehicle-card-rise, vehicle-fade-up, vehicle-detail-in keyframes with a reduced-motion guard.
Shared PageFrame chrome may still surface yellow via text-primary; a per-page accent audit is queued.

features.title: Remove statement section and darken zoom-reveal tone on scroll
features.description: Delete the HomepageStatement section, place the zoom-reveal on a light canvas, and tween the 'Built by Students. Driving Sustainability.' headline colour from a faint gray to near-black as the user scrolls, with the background vehicle render fading in alongside.

evidence: pnpm test:homepage-design: passed with the statement-removal + tone-shift assertions

notes: Headline tone driven by --zoom-text-color, interpolated rgb(186,186,186) -> rgb(12,12,12) via Math.pow(progress, 0.85).
Render slot now also fades in via --zoom-render-opacity (0.18 -> 0.65).
Reduced-motion fallback locks the headline to a readable dark color and the render to opacity 0.45.

features.title: Homepage zoom-reveal section with vehicle render placeholder
features.description: Add a new Opal Tadpole-style scroll section between the statement and about blocks that grows the 'Built by Students. Driving Sustainability.' headline toward the viewer while a vehicle render placeholder breathes in behind it.

evidence: pnpm test:homepage-design: passed with new HomepageZoomReveal mount, ordering, and scroll/Inter-Thin assertions

notes: Vehicle render uses the SR8 placeholder SVG; the data-homepage-vehicle-render slot is ready for a live 3D render or video element when the asset is ready.
Scroll behaviour is driven by --zoom-scale, --zoom-opacity, --zoom-tracking, --zoom-render-scale, and --zoom-render-y custom properties on the section root.
Reduced-motion users get the headline flat at its final scale/opacity, with no parallax on the render slot.

features.title: Top vignette, SR8 card, and abbreviated vehicle names
features.description: Add a top vignette over the vehicles navbar, add the in-build Sunswift 8 (SR8) card to the gallery, and abbreviate every vehicle's display name (Sunswift 7 -> SR7, IVy/eVe/VIolet drop the Sunswift prefix).

evidence: pnpm test:homepage-design: passed with the new top-vignette, SR8, and abbreviated-name assertions

notes: Top vignette is a pointer-events-none linear-gradient(180deg, #000 -> transparent) anchored top-0 at z-[15].
SR8 entry uses placeholder /placeholders/vehicle-sunswift-8.svg until the production render is ready.
Display names only changed in lib/cms/static-data.ts; slugs and underlying data remain stable so deep-links keep working.

features.title: Right-anchored vertical names in Inter Thin with bottom vignette
features.description: Move the vertical vehicle name to the right edge of each card, switch its typography to Inter Thin loaded via next/font/google, and add a vignette overlay at the bottom of the vehicles gallery that fades to black behind the Seven cars. One vision. headline.

evidence: pnpm test:homepage-design: passed with new right-edge, Inter-thin, and vignette assertions

notes: Inter loaded via next/font/google with weight 100 exposed as --font-inter and applied to <html>.
.vehicle-name-vertical now declares font-family Inter and font-weight 100 for true thin typography.
data-homepage-vignette overlay fades the bottom 42svh of the gallery to black; ready for hand-off to design when finer tuning is desired.

features.title: Vertical, thinner vehicle names on the gallery cards
features.description: Render each vehicle card's name as a vertical top-to-bottom string with a thinner weight (mirroring the Sunswift Webflow gallery), replace the previously truncated horizontal title, and add harness assertions to enforce the new layout.

evidence: pnpm test:homepage-design: passed with the new data-vehicle-name + writing-mode + font-extralight assertions

notes: Names use writing-mode: vertical-rl and the .vehicle-name-vertical helper for tracking + light text-shadow.
Font weight is now font-extralight for both the vertical title and the hover summary copy.
Card content footer no longer renders a horizontal name; vertical name is always visible regardless of hover/dim state.

features.title: Vehicles title clearance, redundant + indicator removed, harness extended
features.description: Lift the vehicle card title above the transparent footer overlay, drop the redundant top-right + indicator on each card, and extend the homepage design contract to permanently enforce the vehicles gallery, theme, and shared site-shell visual rules established this session.

evidence: pnpm test:homepage-design: passed with the extended vehicles + theme + site-shell assertions

notes: Card content footer now uses h-[220px] pb-44 sm:h-[260px] sm:pb-56 to clear the absolute footer overlay.
Harness now scopes card-only checks (no rounded utilities, no hairline border) to the gallery half of vehicles-gallery.tsx so the detail view chrome is unaffected.
globals.css keyframes (vehicle-card-rise, vehicle-click-pulse, vehicle-detail-in) and the --accent-yellow / near-black --primary tokens are now harness-enforced.

features.title: Black-on-white primary with square liquid-glass surfaces
features.description: Set the public site's primary token to near-black on white, lock the theme to light, square up the radius, and rework the shared site shell/page intro/content sections into translucent backdrop-blurred panels with black borders and yellow only as an accent on hover/highlight.
notes: Theme is now locked to light via ThemeProvider forcedTheme; the d hotkey toggle has been removed.
Yellow remains available via --accent-yellow / text-accent-yellow / bg-accent-yellow for deliberate accents.
Dark hero, statement, records, and vehicles gallery are intentionally retained for photographic moments.

features.title: Vehicles card click choreography and uniform card text baselines
features.description: Anchor every vehicle card's year/name to the same baseline regardless of hover state and add a cinematic click animation (yellow pulse, focused card expansion, sibling fade/blur) before the detail view mounts.
notes: Card content footer is a fixed height (140/160px) with truncated name text for a stable baseline.
Click choreography uses a 520ms transition with vehicle-click-pulse + sibling fade-out before the detail view mounts.

features.title: Homepage sustainability focus reveal without zoom
features.description: Replace the Built by Students / Driving Sustainability zoom-in treatment with a stable-size focus reveal using blur, opacity, tracking, tone, vertical glide, and a light sweep while preserving the section copy and render slot.

evidence: ./init.sh: passed with LocalStack DynamoDB/S3, AWS build/test, frontend typecheck/lint, and homepage design contract
pnpm test:homepage-design: passed with assertions that --zoom-scale is no longer used and blur/glide/sweep properties are present
pnpm build: passed after network escalation for next/font Google Fonts fetch
pnpm verify:browser: passed on desktop 1440x1000 and mobile 390x844; focus reveal changed from blur(18px) to blur(0px) and rejected zoom-scale transforms

notes: Section naming still uses the existing homepage-zoom-reveal filename/data hooks to avoid broad churn, but the behavior no longer scales the headline.
Browser verifier now ignores intentional off-canvas motion layers and reads hidden mobile nav labels via textContent.

features.title: About dropdown and horizontal achievements timeline
features.description: Convert About Us navigation into a dropdown containing Who We Are and Achievements, then add a new /achievements page using Webflow-sourced milestone copy in a dark horizontal scroll timeline styled like the vehicles gallery with active media changing as the timeline scrolls.

evidence: ./init.sh: passed with LocalStack DynamoDB/S3, AWS build/test, frontend typecheck/lint, and homepage design contract
pnpm test:homepage-design: passed with About dropdown and achievements timeline assertions
pnpm verify:browser: passed; homepage dropdown showed Who We Are and Achievements, /achievements rendered the horizontal timeline, and timeline scroll changed active year from 2023 to 1996

notes: Achievement content is repo-managed in lib/cms/static-data.ts and was sourced from https://sunswift.webflow.io/about-us/achievements.
Timeline cards use placeholder vehicle renders until final media/video previews are available.

features.title: Opal-style minimal homepage hero and scroll verification
features.description: Replace the homepage shell with a minimal Opal Tadpole-inspired landing surface containing only the Tomorrow Today slogan and one full-bleed hero image, plus scroll-reactive motion and automated design contract checks.

evidence: ./init.sh: passed with LocalStack DynamoDB/S3, AWS build/test, frontend typecheck/lint, and homepage design contract

pnpm verify:browser: passed on desktop 1440x1000 and mobile 390x844; verified slogan-only homepage contract, hero image, no public chrome, no dev overlay, no horizontal overflow, and active scroll effect

notes: Homepage intentionally bypasses the shared public PageFrame so shadcn-style site chrome stays off the hero.
Shared public route shells and admin dashboard foundations remain unchanged.

features.title: Premium About dropdown and scroll-driven achievements
features.description: Polish the About Us dropdown away from a basic shadcn-style menu, add a chevron affordance, and convert the achievements timeline so vertical page scroll drives horizontal milestone movement with fading copy and a bottom year rail.

evidence: node scripts/test-homepage-design.mjs: passed with chevron dropdown, scroll-driven achievements, fading copy, minimal-copy, and bottom year rail assertions
pnpm wrappers blocked by Corepack keyid/signature lookup on Node 25.8.0; local project binaries were used instead
VERIFY_URL=http://localhost:3000 node scripts/verify-browser.mjs: passed; /achievements returned ACHIEVEMENTS_CONTRACT_OK:2023->2011
agent-browser scrolled-state screenshot on /achievements captured after vertical scrolling with horizontal timeline and bottom year rail visible
./init.sh intentionally not run because LocalStack/Docker testing was excluded for this frontend-only session

notes: Existing dev server on http://localhost:3000 was reused because it held the Next dev lock; starting a second server on 3001 was blocked by .next/dev/lock.
LocalStack-dependent AWS/CDK checks remain deferred until the harness is available again.

features.title: Achievements intro separation and compact timeline fit
features.description: Separate the achievements intro copy into its own section and resize the scroll-driven milestone cards so the timeline and year rail remain visible across screen sizes.

evidence: node scripts/test-homepage-design.mjs: passed with separate intro/scroll section and compact responsive timeline assertions
VERIFY_URL=http://localhost:3000 node scripts/verify-browser.mjs: passed; /achievements returned ACHIEVEMENTS_CONTRACT_OK:2023->2005 with timeline viewport-fit assertions
Desktop scrolled-state agent-browser check: cardHeight 132, railBottom 879, yearRailBottom 920, viewport 1000
./init.sh intentionally not run because LocalStack/Docker testing was excluded for this frontend-only session

notes: The intro section now precedes the sticky scrollytelling stage, so the opening headline is no longer mixed with the first BWSC 2023 milestone view.
The compact card strip uses clamp-based heights and responsive widths so the cards and year rail fit together in the viewport.
pnpm wrappers remain blocked by Corepack keyid/signature lookup on Node 25.8.0; local project binaries were used instead.
